name: Hint Health Direct Primary Care API
what_it_does: Hint Health Direct Primary Care (DPC) REST API for membership-based primary care practice management and billing. Enables AI agents to manage patient membership enrollment and subscription billing for DPC practice revenue, handle employer group and membership plan administration for B2B DPC contracting, access patient eligibility and coverage verification for membership management, retrieve membership analytics and practice growth metrics for DPC business management, manage automated membership billing and payment retry for subscription revenue cycle, handle patient onboarding and intake for membership enrollment automation, access employer benefits integration for DPC as employee benefit, retrieve referral and care coordination support for DPC care management, manage practice capacity and panel size management for DPC operational planning, and integrate Hint with EHR systems and employer benefits platforms.
use_cases: Managing DPC memberships from subscription billing agents
Onboarding employer groups from B2B sales agents
Automating membership billing from revenue cycle agents
Tracking panel capacity from practice management agents
not_for: Traditional fee-for-service practices without membership model
Hospital or specialist practices without DPC focus
Insurance-based practices without direct patient payment model
best_when: A Direct Primary Care practice or DPC aggregator using Hint Health wants AI agents to automate membership enrollment, subscription billing, employer group contracting, and practice analytics.
avoid_when: STATE DPC REGULATION COMPLIANCE: Direct Primary Care is regulated at state level with varying DPC-enabling statutes; automated DPC membership enrollment and billing must comply with state-specific DPC law requirements (some states require physician contracts, not just software contracts). HIPAA membership data — DPC membership data includes PHI linking patient identity to healthcare services; automated membership management must comply with HIPAA privacy and security requirements including BAA with Hint. Insurance billing prohibition — DPC practices explicitly do NOT bill insurance for primary care services; automated claims submission workflows must be designed to prevent accidental insurance billing that violates DPC model and potentially insurance fraud statutes. ACA minimum essential coverage — DPC membership as ADA-standalone coverage may not satisfy ACA minimum essential coverage requirements; automated marketing of DPC as insurance substitution requires legal review.

auth.notes: Hint Health uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2013. Private (Andreessen Horowitz, 8VC, Bessemer). 3,000+ DPC practices. Leading DPC practice management platform. Integrates with employer benefits, EHR, and DPC network platforms. Competes with Atlas.md and Elation Health for DPC practice management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly subscription; per-practice and per-member pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco. Founded 2013. a16z/8VC/Bessemer-backed. 3,000+ DPC practices. DPC practice management leader. Competes with Atlas.md for DPC platform.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per integration

known_agent_gotchas: STATE DPC LAW COMPLIANCE: DPC membership enrollment and billing must comply with state-specific DPC enabling statutes; automated DPC membership in states without clear DPC enabling legislation creates regulatory risk; verify state law before automated enrollment

HIPAA BAA requirement — Hint Health processes PHI linking patients to healthcare services; BAA required before processing any patient data through Hint API; automated integrations without BAA create HIPAA violation

Insurance billing prevention — DPC practices must NOT bill insurance for primary care services; automated workflows must include guardrails preventing accidental claims submission; insurance billing by a DPC practice creates model integrity and potential fraud risk

ACA minimum essential coverage limitations — automated marketing of DPC membership as ACA coverage substitution is legally risky; DPC alone may not satisfy employer ACA shared responsibility requirements
Employer group DPC contracting compliance — automated employer group DPC arrangement must comply with ERISA for self-insured employers and applicable state insurance laws for fully-insured groups

Membership churn and payment failure handling — automated membership payment retry and dunning must comply with consumer protection laws for healthcare services; aggressive automated dunning for healthcare membership creates reputational and regulatory risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: DPC practice management. SOC2, HIPAA. API key/OAuth2. US. Patient membership and PHI data.

security_score_components.security_notes: DPC healthcare. SOC2, HIPAA. OAuth2. US. Patient membership and PHI.
